title: Dashboard metrics show 'No Data' for last 24 hours
content:
**Symptom:** One or more dashboard panels display 'No Data' or a flat line despite resources being active in your cloud account.

**Step 1 — Check integration health:**
   Navigate to Settings → Integrations. If any integration shows a warning or error icon,    resolve that first (see ERR-4012 for AWS credential issues).

**Step 2 — Verify the metric namespace:**
   For AWS, some metrics (e.g., ECS container metrics) require the CloudWatch Agent    to be installed on the host and are not available via the standard CloudWatch API.    Check Settings → Integrations → AWS → Enhanced Monitoring.

**Step 3 — Check time-zone alignment:**
   CloudDash stores metrics in UTC. If your dashboard's time picker is set to a local    timezone with a large offset, you may be viewing a future time range.    Set the picker to 'Last 3 hours' to confirm data is arriving.

**Step 4 — Check plan limits:**
   Starter plan accounts are limited to 50 monitored resources. If you have exceeded    this limit, newer resources are silently excluded. Navigate to Settings → Usage to    view your resource count.

**Step 5 — Wait for backfill:**
   After reconnecting an integration, CloudDash backfills up to 72 hours of historical    data. This process can take 15–30 minutes for large accounts.
